<!-- Generated by striatum 1.14.0. Regenerate with `striatum skills install`. -->
# Striatum Agent Guide

This guide is the generic-profile equivalent of the Claude Code
skill bundle. Concatenate it into your agent's system prompt or
`cat` it before driving a Striatum workflow.

Striatum is a local-first runner whose live state is
`.striatum/state.sqlite3` in the target repository. The runner —
not your prose — advances state.

## Scaffold

Bring a target repo from "no `.striatum/`" to "a run that's ready
to claim work":

- `striatum init` — Create `.striatum/state.sqlite3` in the target repo.
- `striatum workflow init` — Scaffold a starter `workflow.json` + roles/prompts.
- `striatum workflow validate` — Validate a workflow file; non-zero exit on error.
- `striatum run prepare` — Snapshot the workflow and prepare a run id.
- `striatum run start` — Transition a prepared run to `running`.
- `striatum branch confirm` — Confirm or create the run's working branch.

Common pattern:

```bash
striatum --repo /path/to/target init
striatum --repo /path/to/target workflow validate path/to/workflow.json
striatum --repo /path/to/target run prepare --workflow path/to/workflow.json --json
striatum --repo /path/to/target run start --run-id <id> --json
```

## Claim loop

The core verb sequence for actually doing work:

- `striatum register-session` — Open an agent session for a (role, lane) pair.
- `striatum claim-next` — Claim the next ready work packet for a session.
- `striatum ack` — Acknowledge a claimed packet and start work.
- `striatum heartbeat` — Extend the active lease while work continues.
- `striatum publish-artifact` — Publish a build artifact for a job.
- `striatum verdict` — Record a non-finding verdict for a review job.
- `striatum submit-review` — Publish a finding artifact + verdict in one call.
- `striatum complete` — Mark a non-review job complete.
- `striatum worktree create` — Create the per-job git worktree (RFC 0008).
- `striatum worktree release` — Release a per-job worktree when work is done.

Common pattern:

```bash
striatum --repo $REPO register-session --run-id $RUN \
    --role $ROLE --lane $LANE --json
striatum --repo $REPO claim-next --session-id $SESSION --json
striatum --repo $REPO ack --session-id $SESSION \
    --message-id $MSG --lease-id $LEASE --json
striatum --repo $REPO publish-artifact --session-id $SESSION \
    --job-id $JOB --lease-id $LEASE \
    --kind $KIND --logical-name $NAME --path $PATH --json
striatum --repo $REPO complete --session-id $SESSION \
    --job-id $JOB --lease-id $LEASE --json
```

For review jobs, replace the last two steps with
`submit-review --verdict {accept,accept_with_findings,needs_revision,reject}`.

## Front-matter kinds

When publishing Markdown artifacts whose kind has a registered
schema, include the front matter the publisher expects. The
allowed kinds in this runner version are:

- action_item_ledger
- decision
- finding
- findings_ledger
- handoff
- harness_improvement_proposal
- marker
- other
- patch_summary
- prompt
- support_ledger
- synthesis
- test_report

The publisher refuses invalid front matter with exit code 6.

## Long-lived supervised lanes

If the lane's harness profile sets
`supervision.compatible: true`, packets arrive as newline-
terminated JSON on stdin via `striatum supervise send`:

- `striatum supervise start` — Start a long-lived agent CLI for the session.
- `striatum supervise send` — Deliver a packet to a supervisor's stdin pipe.
- `striatum supervise stop` — Stop a supervisor (SIGTERM, SIGKILL after 5s).
- `striatum supervise status` — Probe a supervisor's liveness.
- `striatum supervise list` — List supervisors for a run.

## Recovery

When a run looks stuck:

- `striatum status` — Read run/job/blocker state.
- `striatum why` — Explain why a job is in its current state.
- `striatum doctor --verbose` — Surface structured consistency problems.
- `striatum recovery stale-leases` — Lazy-expire and report stale leases.
- `striatum recovery requeue-stale` — Requeue an expired non-repo-write job.
- `striatum recovery process-reconcile` — Reconcile externally-killed processes.
- `striatum checkpoint resolve` — Resolve a `human_checkpoint` blocker.
- `striatum dashboard --once` — Render a single dashboard frame for scripts.

Common pattern:

```bash
striatum --repo $REPO status --run-id $RUN --json
striatum --repo $REPO why $JOB_OR_RUN --json
striatum --repo $REPO doctor --run-id $RUN --verbose --json
```

## What not to do

- Do not write to `.striatum/state.sqlite3` directly; the runner is the only writer (D006/D009).
- Do not treat marker files, tmux panes, or terminal output as workflow state.
- Do not advance state by printing phrases; advance by calling the CLI verbs in your work packet.
- Do not capture stdout/stderr transcripts; D028 keeps them off by default.
- Do not derive bylines from job titles; use the byline supplied in your work packet.
- Do not parse a supervisor's own output for workflow state; supervisors send DEVNULL.
